entry.title: Continuous Feedback Details Delete Button
entry.content:
After 1H.2026 release, I noticed a Delete Feedback button is available to all users in their Delete Feedback Details. According to the resource below, "In a recent enhancement, administrators can Disable Deleting Feedback, so users are not going to be able to delete any feedback received, even if they requested it themselves. The Delete option would be totally removed from Continuous Feedback menu, for end users. The Disable Deleting Feedback option is available on the Continuous Performance Management Configuration page."

Now what is weird, is I have Disabled Deleting Feedback in Production CPM configurations. However, in my Test environment I don't find the option to disable in CPM Configurations. My fear is when the release hits Prod, I won't be able to disable.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-successfactors-performance-management-administration/introducing-continuous-feedback

Is anyone else experiencing the option to disable is no longer available after the 1H.2026 release in their Preview environments?

entry.title: During the Hiring Process
entry.content:
It’s time for the hiring process! We have already reviewed a few measures you can take to detect potential application fraud during the candidate selection and before the interview. Now, let’s see how SAP Fieldglass features can help you during your hiring process. 

Work Order Approval: Segregation of Duties 

During your Work Order Approval workflow, you can include additional steps to enhance security during your hiring process, such as multiple approvals, comments, and ratings. 

These are some benefits of taking these actions: 

  • Requiring approvals from multiple parties reduces the risk that a single individual approves fraudulent or incorrect work orders. 
  • Mandating comments forces approvers to actively engage with the work order, consider its details, and justify their decisions, making it less likely that approvals are given without careful consideration. 
  • Providing ratings offers a quick, quantifiable assessment of the work order's quality, necessity, and compliance, helping to flag potentially risky or non-compliant work orders for further review. 
  • Requiring documented feedback (comments and ratings) from approvers creates an audit trail that promotes accountability.  

By incorporating these elements into your Work Order Approval workflow, you improve the security and integrity of your hiring process in SAP Fieldglass. 

Interview Upload 

Another feature that can help you detect potential fraud during a selection process is the Interview Upload. 

The Interview Upload feature helps you to verify the candidate's identity by recording interview details linked to specific candidates. This creates an audit trail that matches uploaded interviews to applicant profiles. The system can detect multiple interviews for the same position submitted under different identities and flag suspicious names or ID mismatches that might indicate fraudulent submissions. 

Also, it helps prevent submission of pre-recorded or fabricated interviews, enabling verification of interview dates against application timelines. This documentation helps confirm that the interviews are genuine and submitted at the appropriate time in the hiring process. 

Uploaded interviews serve as permanent records that can be reviewed at any time you need. This transparency ensures that hiring decisions are based on verifiable, legitimate information. 

Using the Interview Upload feature, you can create a more secure, transparent, and efficient hiring process.

Job Seeker Review 

The Job Seeker Review tool is designed to simplify the evaluation and selection process for job seekers who have applied to job postings. It can help you detect candidate fraud during the hiring process. 

Using AI, the Job Seeker Review can validate skills, identify inconsistencies, and flag potential risks in submitted resumes and profiles. It helps you identify discrepancies between a candidate's claimed experience and the job requirements. 

You can also rate job seekers, compare and score them. 

SAP Fieldglass's Job Seeker Review combines machine learning, identity verification, and historical tracking to build a comprehensive fraud-detection system. You can reduce risk, save time and resources, and ultimately hire candidates you can trust.  

Smart verification isn't just efficient, it's essential in today's hiring processes.  

Now that you know some features that can help you during your hiring process, let’s see next how SAP Fieldglass features can help you during the offer and start stages. Don’t miss it! 

 

 

For the purposes of this guide, “candidate fraud” means deliberate misrepresentation of identity, credentials, work history, or qualifications during recruitment. This guide is preventive and educational; it does not make legal determinations.

entry.title: Standard Warranty Module - Action A200 Dump Issue
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts

If a customer wants to order a part to perform a self-repair on a finished product that is in warranty, how should we handle such a scenario in SAP?

Currently, we are using the master warranty as a (warranty) BOM to display warranty coverage and parts that can be sold separately later to the customer. Now, while using the standard SAP warranty module and executing action A200 for warranty check, we are getting an ABAP dump. Need guidance on how we should set up our master data correctly so we do not get this dump.

entry.title: 1H 2026 SuccessFactors Onboarding Enhancement: Predelivered Custom Task Template Objects
entry.content:
Managing custom tasks in your onboarding and offboarding processes just got significantly easier. The 1H 2026 release introduces a game-changing enhancement: predelivered custom task template objects that eliminate the manual setup burden and accelerate your time to value.

The Challenge: Manual Configuration Overhead

Previously, when organizations wanted to capture custom data for their onboarding or offboarding tasks, they had to manually create custom MDF (Master Data Framework) objects for each custom task. This process was:

- Time-consuming – Admins spent considerable time designing and creating MDF objects from scratch

- Error-prone – Manual configuration increased the risk of setup mistakes

- Complex – Required deep technical knowledge of MDF object structure and configuration

- Repetitive – Similar custom task requirements across organizations meant duplicating the same effort

 

The Solution: Ready-to-Use Template Objects

We're excited to introduce an increase of the Onboarding-specific MDF limit up to 500, as well as the concept of predelivered custom task template objects that are now available out-of-the-box in SuccessFactors Onboarding. These templates provide ready-made MDF objects specifically designed for custom tasks, allowing you to:

 

Get Started Faster

No more starting from scratch. Select from a library of predelivered template objects that are immediately available in your system. Simply choose the template that matches your needs and configure your custom task in minutes, not hours.

 

Reduce Configuration Errors

Each template object is pre-configured with the optimal structure for custom task data collection, including:

- Proper field definitions and data types

- Correct security settings (RBP-secured with subject user field)

- Built-in searchability for key fields (activity ID, process ID, subject user)

- Appropriate permission categories

- The following field types are supported:

  • Boolean

  • Date

  • DateTime

  • Decimal

  • Generic Object

  • Number

  • String

  • Time

  • User


The following field character limits apply:

Custom FieldCharacter Limit

String + Users

40

Generic Object + Number + Boolean

20

Date + Time + DateTime

10

Decimal

10


Simplified Administration

The predelivered templates eliminate the complexity of MDF object creation. Administrators can now focus on defining the business logic of their custom tasks rather than wrestling with technical configuration details.

 

Flexibility for Both Onboarding and Offboarding

All template objects support both onboarding and offboarding processes, giving you maximum flexibility to use them across your entire employee lifecycle.

 

How It Works

The predelivered custom task template objects are automatically available in your 1H 2026 environment through a feature toggle that's enabled by default. When creating a custom task definition, you can now:

  1. Select from predelivered templates – Browse the available template objects in the Custom Tasks settings
  2. Associate the template – Link the template object to your custom task definition
  3. Configure your task – Define task name, description, module, and other properties
  4. Start using immediately – The template handles all the underlying data structure

 

Each template object provides:

- Dedicated database tables for optimal performance

- Reportable data through the Onboarding2 schema

- API support for integration scenarios

- Full localization support for global deployments

 

Seamless Integration with Existing Workflows

These predelivered templates work seamlessly with all existing custom task functionality, including:

- Custom task assignment and delegation

- Task completion workflows

- Notification templates

- Reporting and analytics

- REST APIs for custom task operations

 

Getting Started

For detailed information on creating and configuring custom tasks with the new predelivered template objects, please refer to the SAP Help documentation: Creating Custom MDF Object for Custom Tasks

 

Looking Ahead

This enhancement represents our commitment to simplifying administration while maintaining the flexibility and power that SuccessFactors Onboarding customers depend on. By providing predelivered templates, we're reducing the barrier to entry for custom tasks and enabling more organizations to take advantage of this powerful capability.

We're excited to see how you use these new templates to streamline your onboarding and offboarding processes!

 

---

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
// Instead of writing this ...
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( bufferSize + 1 );
// write this ...
// Note that some ODBC drivers have a 2-byte SQLWCHAR (like us) and 
// some ODBC drivers have a 4-byte SQLWCHAR, so write this.
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeof(SQLWCHAR);
// Example: for CHAR(10) data, characterSize = 10 and 
// the + 1 accounts for a null terminator.
// Analogously, the following works for non-wide SQLCHAR.
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeof(SQLCHAR);
// To generalize the "BufferLength" value ...
characterSize = n; // Ex: 10 for SQL CHAR(10) or VARCHAR(10)
sizeofCharacter = sizeof(SQLWCHAR); //or sizeof(SQLCHAR)
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( characterSize + 1 ) * sizeofCharacter;

entry.title: Re: Automate BTP Custom Domain certificates using Let's Encrypt
entry.content:
Hi @Ben ,

 

thanks for this awesome blog. I just found it as I am building a similar solution, renewing a certificate in the custom domain service within a gitlab pipeline.

I just came across the issue that the http challenge seems to be only working when already a valid certificate is deployed for the domain. During the initial setup this is not the case. Calling the /.well-known/... endpoint via http redirects to https and in that case the token is delivered but with an invalid certificate (CNAME still the cf....ondemand.com). Did you find any solution to use the let's encrypt stuff also for the initial verification? As said, as soon as a valid certificate is deployed once, renewing is not an issue.  But in the initial stage my script is just hanging at the point "Waiting for verification ..." with never coming to a response.

Or is this also the reason why you described in the git repository that the initial onboarding for a domain includes once the SSL setup manually?

 

Thanks for an update and cheers,

Michael

entry.title: A memorable SAP Community gathering at the SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026
entry.content:
The SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026 was truly a remarkable event, bringing together an impressive lineup of speakers and community members. It marked a special moment for the SAP ecosystem in Brazil, especially with the first visit of SAP Developer Advocates to the country. 

We had the honor of welcoming @noravonthenen @thomas_jung @RichHeilman @Vitaliy-R and SAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley, although Antonia is based in Brazil for some time, originally comes from Germany. Their presence, along with the broader SAP Community in Brazil, made this event in Campinas even more meaningful.

This visit was part of an extensive SAP CodeJam Roadshow across Brazil. For the first time, the country hosted more than 17 CodeJam sessions, hands-on training events focused on SAP development. It was an impressive milestone, and you can check the full events agenda in Brazil through this link (SIT + Codejam + SAP Meet ups).

The event itself took place at FACAMP, a beautiful university campus surrounded by green areas and open spaces. The day started with a warm welcome coffee break, followed by a full agenda that included lunch and additional networking breaks.

The opening session featured Josh and Antonia Zorn the SAP Community, followed by Thomas Jung and Rich, who spoke about ABAP AI topics that have been gaining strong traction in recent CodeJams. The agenda continued with several high-quality sessions from speakers like Vitaliy, Nora, @Secco , Leandro Dante, @MRobalinho among others. I’ve included links below so you can watch all sessions on YouTube.

 

Session Title Speaker(s)

ABAP AI — Artificial Intelligence in ABAP DevelopmentSAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley
SAP Developer Advocates @thomas_jung @RichHeilman
Tax Reform — 2026 RoadmapRoberto Paiva
GenAI — A Practical View of Generative AI in the SAP Ecosystem@noravonthenen
AI in SAP HANA Cloud@Vitaliy-R
Automatic PIX Implementation@MRobalinho 
AI, Geopolitics, and the IT Bubble — The SAP Architecture Market OutlookMarcelo Figueiredo Cabrera
From Data to Decisions — Accelerating Results with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)Renata Cristina
Transformation Focused on Business Value GenerationRodrigo Cordeiro
SAP BTP in Practice — Integrating Solutions and Connecting SAP ProductsDiego Yegros
SAP BTP Multitenancy in Practice — From Add-On to the FutureJoão Luiz Martins
GROW Retail Fashion — Architecture, Scalability, and Changes in S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition)Diego Bortolucci
LLM, Joule, and RPT-1 — The New AI Stack for Structured Data in SAPLeandro Dante
AI for Functional Consultants — From Basic GPT to Prototyping@Secco 
Giveaways & Raffles — Closing SessionOrganization

One of the highlights of the event was its relaxed and friendly atmosphere. Attendees felt comfortable engaging with speakers, taking photos, and networking throughout the day. Unlike many events where people attend only a few sessions, most participants stayed until the very end, clear evidence of the quality and engagement of the content.

For me personally, it was also a special experience. I had the opportunity to contribute as a volunteer, supporting the live streaming of the event. While that meant I couldn’t take as many photos as I would have liked, it was incredibly rewarding to reconnect with friends and meet so many great professionals from the SAP community. 

Campinas continues to be a key hub for SAP events, attracting not only local participants but also many attendees from São Paulo. Alongside other cities like Ribeirão Preto and São José dos Campos, it plays an important role in strengthening the SAP ecosystem in the region.

 If you are an SAP professional, I strongly recommend keeping an eye on upcoming SAP Inside Track events in your area. Follow the SAP Community São Paulo group on LinkedIn and stay connected, you don’t want to miss these opportunities for learning, networking, and reconnecting with amazing people in the SAP world.

Special thanks to @RobertaFernande and the organizing team for making this event possible.

Feel free to share your own photos and experiences, or check out the SAP Inside Track LinkedIn page for more highlights from the event. The level of community engagement and social media activity around this event was truly outstanding.

It was a pleasure to be part of SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026. Looking forward to seeing you all again at future events.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Decommissioning Procedure & FAQ
entry.content:
@kenjodrey6 That's correct; the Standard Export Job should remember where it left off, and continue exporting from there.

@paul_bracy I'd refer you to the Onboarding 1.0 API protocols: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-onboarding/overview-of-onboarding-1-0-and-offboarding-1-0-api/change-history?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

@evgeniia_kuzn answering your questions here but adding this to the FAQ:

  • Will SuperAdmin be available after May 14, 2026?
    • SuperAdmin will still be accessible after this time. It will be shut down and access removed between 1H 2026 and 1H 2027. However, accounts will be "locked" and normal operations will not be possible on them.
  • Is it a must to turn off all standard exports in SuperAdmin and clean up CPI/Boomi?
    • Once these jobs are all 'caught up' then yes, I'd recommend deactivating them and setting the various Features for an ONB1 tenant to inactive. This is not strictly required however.
  • Will old candidate's links to Onboarding Employee Portal and to Complete Paperwork (NES) still work after May 14th and until final purge?
    • These will no longer function.

entry.title: Re: Query Report Tips Part 1 - How to Display PO ME23N in SQ01
entry.content:
You don't even need to put in SU01. You can put the following code in the Code Section: DATA

DATA: zfield(1) TYPE c.
zfield = 'X'.
SET PARAMETER ID 'ENJ_CALL_ME23N' FIELD zfield.

Now add Transaction ME23 as Report Assignment in your report.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition - The “Trust Layer” for Agentic AI
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

1) Speed to value: AI that accelerates how integrations are built

Enhanced edition includes AI features aimed at boosting developer productivity, including:

  • Text‑to‑Flow generation — build integration flows using a natural language interface.
  • Script Optimization — analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript scripts inside iFlows.

In an agentic world, integration teams must iterate faster — because agents will continuously evolve how they use tools, call APIs, and respond to events. These capabilities are about compressing the distance between an idea and a governed, deployable integration asset.

2) Operational trust: AI that helps integrations run with confidence

Agents introduce new scale and new patterns — and that means you need sharper operational insight. Enhanced edition includes AI-driven operations efficiency features such as:

  • API Anomaly Detection — AI-driven observability that detects unusual behavior and flags issues early.
  • API Traffic Prediction — identify trends and predict upcoming API call volumes to support smarter planning and load management.

This is what makes the positioning “agentic enabler” real: it’s not just that AI helps you build integrations — it helps you run the execution fabric with the resilience and foresight required for autonomous action.

"

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing again, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Q1 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Register for our Q1 2026 Service Solutions webcast

2026 will prove to be a transformational year for many companies as AI assistants and agents drive high value in service delivery. I’m already excited about the Q2 Innovations blog! Get registered and ready for a preview at Sapphire 2026 of how SAP’s embedded AI innovations are delivering value for customers every day. But first things first, let me highlight some key innovations delivered in Q1 for SAP Enterprise Service Management (SAP ESM).

Ensuring seamless look and feel throughout a unified workspace

Delivering the right user experience has high impact on value and adoption for service specialists especially when the ESM workspace includes third party applications and processes. Using mashups is the preferred method to create an integrated environment ensuring service representatives don’t have to login to different systems or open sperate windows. Now the configuration for mashups has been extended to include theme parameters. With this setting the embedded mashup will adjust automatically to the system set theme providing a consistent look and feel for SAP and 3rd party embedded solutions.

Deliver connected experiences and activity across solutions

The event hub service in BTP allows solutions to publish inbound and outbound events ensuring others can access these activities for visibility and execution of line of business specific processes. SAP ESM now publishes outbound events to the SAP BTP Cloud Application Event Hub so other applications  receive them ensuring a unified and transparent experience across systems. This simplifies the need for system-to-system integration and centralizes events. This transparency can be realized with SAP SuccessFactors ensuring case updates are provided on employee service interactions directly on the SuccessFactors home page.

Self-Service available anytime, anywhere

Employees can get instant answers and create cases from the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app providing easy 24/7 access for HR support. This provides greater flexibility and ease of access for HR related inquiries and gives employees the ability to work from their channel of choice, even if they are on the go. HR Case management is available as well, allowing creation of new cases, viewing and updating existing cases from wherever they are. Additionally, employees receive notifications when action is needed, ensuring case resolution without response delays.

Accelerating case resolution with UX simplicity

An approval process can delay case resolutions. Working with our customers’ inputs, several improvements have been made that reduce time, ensure consistency and enable a more streamlined handling of cases where approvals are needed. Ensuring quick steps to final resolution, both Service reps and managers benefit from a more efficient process and consistency while approvals are in progress. The following capabilities have been enabled to accelerate the approval process:

  • Approval steps enabled in auto flow scenarios
  • Case status triggers notifications or email
  • Directly approve from case header
  • Easy access to visibility in next steps and status
  • Read-only case state during approval steps


Flexing AI to accelerate case resolution

Another key improvement in Q1 includes extending the business information extraction capability to include custom fields. This capability saves time, reduces errors and delivers ease to service teams – eliminating the need to scour through email for relevant information and then having to make several manual entries. SAP to SAP solutions is rather logical, but often there are custom fields or 3rd party solutions. Now with custom fields included there’s additional flexibility ensuring service reps have improved automation, prepossessing and context to quickly close cases.

 
 

 

More Information & Thank you!

For additional Q1 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management, reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q1 Release Webinar, and to keep up as we release capabilities each month reference the What’s New in our help portal. Please also reference these valuable links for more on SAP’s CX Customer Service Solutions:

SAP learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Innovation Spotlight Webinar

To learn more about our Q1/2026 innovations across SAP’s CX solutions check out the blog!

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics!

Finally, an enormous thank you to this expanding community, for your active participation and collaboration; as we all benefit – SAP employees, partners and customers -  from sharing our experiences and information on SAP ESM.

entry.title: Spring is Here, NYC SAP Community: Let's Meet Up! ☀️
entry.content:
Hey lovely community,

Can we just pause for a minute to admire this weather? After the lengthy New York winter, these warm spring days are really something else. I've been grabbing every chance to go to the park (to run of course :D) and just a leisurely afternoon with my dog and some SAP documentation on the laptop (yes, that's me, the guy). The park is always a big yes for me. 🌲



Which is why I'm actually reaching out: it's time for our next NYC SAP meetup, and I would like to get your thoughts.Here are a couple of simple questions for you👇

1. When do you prefer?

Are we a weeknight-after-work crowd, or do people like a weekend daytime vibe?
Specifically:Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday evenings? Friday happy hour? Saturday afternoon in the park?

2. What time of day?

Early evening (6–8 PM) after work?
Later evening drinks (7:30 PM onward)? Afternoon / daytime on a weekend?

3. Indoor or outdoor where?

For me, with the weather being like this, I'd really fancy an outdoor activity: a park hangout, picnic-style, maybe Central Park like the first time, or somewhere in NJ because a lot of you are there.
However, if people around would rather a rooftop, a pub, or a coffee, let's hear it.

Comment below Let me know what suits you — day, time, and vibe.

According to what the majority says, I'll come up with a plan and we'll schedule a date. Can hardly wait to finally put faces to some of you (and seeing again those of you I haven't met for a while). Let's make it a great one.

See you soon ❤️ hopefully in a park! 🌳

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing your perspective RubikWal. A few points I would like to share here - 

1. While SAP Integration Suite prioritizes a stateless architecture to ensure high performance and maintain 'Clean Core' principles, it does provides robust persistence capabilities through JMS Queues and Data Stores. 

2. In JMS Queues, in case of failures, we can see all the failed messages in the dead letter queue through in-built monitoring capability, plus we can select any of the failed message we want to reprocess and move it manually to process to backend.

3. Beyond JMS, SAP has AEM that takes it further, by offering Message Replay replay and enhanced visibility beyond standard JMS. 

4. To address the point on 'half-ready' error handling, SAP provides Standard Integration Flows for A2A and B2B scenarios that include out-of-the-box retry mechanisms. By leveraging Partner Directory , developers can simply configure connectivity for partners and applications rather than building from scratch. Furthermore, the platform offers the flexibility to customize error logic via Exception Sub-processes, ensuring that complex requirements are met.

entry.title: Re: Validation in 'Next Posting Date' in Tcode-F110
entry.content:
Some options are

1) Payment Proposal Review:
Ensure that the proposal run is checked before the payment run. The proposal will show invoices with future posting dates, and you can exclude them manually.

2) Payment Run Parameters:
In F110, the "Posting Date for Payment Document" is set during the run. If this is earlier than the invoice posting date, SAP still allows it. You can restrict the "Next Posting Date" so that it doesn’t extend beyond the current period. This is a configuration setting in the payment run parameters.

3) Payment Method Configuration (FBZP):
Few clients handle this by restricting payment methods to only pick invoices that are due within the current posting period. In FBZP, under payment method settings, you can control "Earliest Due Date" logic.

In short, without enhancements, the most effective control is to tighten F110 parameters and payment block usage. That way, invoices dated in April won’t be picked up in a March run.

entry.title: Re: How to prevent clearing Vendor Payment with another V...
entry.content: SAP does not allow clearing invoices of one vendor against another in standard functionality. If you are seeing this, it is either a master data setup (head office/branch, alternate payee) or user selection issue. To prevent it, review vendor master relationships and tighten clearing parameters/authorizations.

entry.title: SAP AI CoreをClineと連携してVibe Codingしてみよう!
entry.content:
はじめに

近年、生成AIの急速な進化により、ソフトウェア開発の現場は大きな転換期を迎えています。GitHub Copilot や Cursor に代表されるAIコーディングアシスタントが普及する中、タスクを自律的に計画・実行できるエージェント型AIツールへと進化が進んでいます。Clineはその代表格であり、VS Code上で AI が第二の開発者（いや、第一？）として機能します。いわゆる Vibe Coding というやつです。

Cline では API Provider （Google や Open AI, Anthropic 等）を選択して生成AIモデルを利用しますが、その API Provider として SAP AI Core を選択することができます。本ブログでは、SAP AI Core を Cline と連携し、Generative AI Hub にデプロイされた生成AIモデルを利用する方法をご紹介します。

 

前提条件

本ブログは、以下の内容を前提条件として構成しています。

  • SAP AI CoreのサービスをBTP Cloud Foundry環境で有効化済み
  • VS Codeをインストール済み

ローカルのシステム情報

本ブログは、以下のシステム上で動作を確認しています。

  • OS -- macOS Tahoe 26.3.1
  • VS Code -- Version: 1.109.5

 

設定手順

1. VS Code の拡張機能「Cline」をインストール

VS Codeを開きます。

1. 左側のパネルから "Extensions" を押下します。

2. 検索バーにて "cline" を検索します。

3. "Cline" を開きます。

4. "Install" を押下して、拡張機能「Cline」をインストールします。

2. Cline の設定画面を開く

Clineのインストールが完了すると、左側のパネルに Cline のアイコンが表示されます。

1. アイコンをクリックして、Cline の画面を開きます。

2. 右上の設定アイコンから Settings 画面を開きます。

3. API Provider で "SAP AI Core" 選択します。

3. SAP AI Core の Client Credentials を取得する

Cline の API Configuration タブから API Provider に SAP AI Core を選択すると、SAP AI Core のクレデンシャルが求められます。

BTP Cockpitへアクセスし、Client Credentials を取得します。

1. BTP Cockpit - Subaccount の Services > Service Marketplace から SAP AI Core のサービスを作成します。

 

2. 下記項目を選択、入力して SAP AI Core のインスタンスを作成します。

  • Service: SAP AI Core
  • Plan: extended
    ※ LLMへのアクセスが必要なため、extended planが必須です。
  • Runtime Environments: Cloud Foundry
  • Space: <任意のスペース>
  • Instance Name: <任意のインスタンス名>

 

3. 2で作成した、SAP AI Core のインスタンスのビューページへアクセスし、サービスキーを作成します。

 

4. 下記項目を入力してサービスキーを作成します。

  • Service Key Name: <任意のサービスキー名>

 

5. 4で作成したサービスキー名を押下し、発行されたサービスキーを表示します。

 

6. VS Code の Cline > API Configuration にサービスキーの Client Credentials を入力します。

Cline > API ConfigurationSAP AI Core > Service key
AI Core Client Idclient id
AI Core Client Secretclientsecret
AI Core Base URLserviceurls/AI_API_URL
AI Core Auth URLurl
AI Core Resource Group"default"

 

4. Clineを使用する

「3. SAP AI Core の Client Credentials を取得する」ステップが完了すると、Generative AI Hub にデプロイされている生成AIモデルが読み込まれます。"Model" プルダウンから使用するモデルを選択し、設定を完了します。

 

ここまでで Cline で SAP AI Core を使うための設定が終了しました。

それでは実際に Cline を使ってみましょう！

今回は例として、以下のプロンプトを実行します。

  • "Hello world !!!"を標準出力するプログラムをpythonで実装して

 

すぐにPythonでの実装が確認できました。

 

終わりに

本ブログでは、SAP AI Core を Cline から使用する方法についてご紹介しました。

SAP AI Core の Client Credentials を Cline 側で設定することで、Generative AI Hub にデプロイされている生成AIモデルが利用できます。

みなさんも Cline を活用して、Vibe Coding Life を楽しみましょう！

entry.title: Re: How to handle multiple cost categories per PO line in...
entry.content:
No, the standard Reconcile GR/IR Accounts Fiori app (F3302) in SAP S/4HANA does not support splitting a single PO line into multiple analytical items by cost category. The app reconciles at the PO line level. So freight, customs, and material bundled into one line will always appear together. To achieve category-level separation, you need either PO design changes (separate line items per category) or custom enhancements.

Below are some options to achieve Category-Level separation

1) Best Practice - Separate PO Lines
Create distinct PO lines for material, freight, customs, etc. Each line then appears separately in GR/IR reconciliation. This is the cleanest way to align with different workflows, aging logic, and responsible teams.

2) Account Assignment Split
Use different account assignment categories or valuation classes for each cost component. This allows reporting separation in FI/CO, though the GR/IR app still shows one PO line.

3) Enhancement Options
Custom CDS views: Extend the GR/IR reconciliation app with a custom analytical view that splits by cost category field (if available in PO or invoice data).
BAdIs / Enhancements: Implement logic to derive and display subcategories in a custom Fiori app or extension of F3302.
Workflow routing: Use workflow rules based on account assignment or G/L account to direct items to different teams, even if they appear as one line in the app.

entry.title: Time Valuation for 4 Cycle Shifts | SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a requirement for Ireland involving a 4‑cycle clock-time–based work schedule (for example, 4 Cycle A), which spans 28 days across 4 weeks. In this schedule:

  • Week 1: Employees work 60 hours
  • Week 2: Employees work 24 hours
  • …and the pattern continues for the remaining weeks.

    From the 60 hours worked in Week 1:

    • 40 hours are paid as Basic hrs
    • The remaining 20 hours are split as follows:
      • 2 hours are paid as Overtime @ 1.5
      • 16 hours are carried forward to Week 2 and added to its 24 Basic hours, totaling 40 Basic hours
      • The remaining 2 hours (from the original 20) are paid as Overtime @ 1.5 in Week 2

This carryover logic continues for the following weeks in the cycle.

Question:
How can I retrieve the number of hours worked from the previous week in the Time Valuation so that these hours can be used for the Basic pay and Overtime calculation in the current week?

Thanks,
Mohd Saif Ansari

4 Cycle A Work Schedule:

entry.title: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content:
In the legacy People Profile of SAP SuccessFactors, the position code was displayed alongside the position name, making it easy for consultants and HR users to identify positions quickly.

However, in the latest People Profile (introduced after last upgrades), only the position name is displayed. The position code is no longer visible in the profile header, making it time-consuming for users to retrieve this information from the Position Org Chart.

To address this limitation, I explored a workaround solution that enables displaying the position code again in the latest People Profile header.

Legacy people profile – Earlier position code is available beside of the position name


Latest people profile- Only the position name is displayed, and the position code is no longer visible in the header.

Workaround Solution:
Step 1: Go to configure latest people profile > General settings


Step 2: Click on configure header fields

Step 3: Select first employee information type as Position

If Position Field Is Not Available in Dropdown - It needs to be synced to a UDF using HRIS Sync Mapping., For mapping Position field the UDF please follow the below steps.
3.1. Go to the Manage Business Configuration > Job Information > Position Field > Click on details

3.2. Add an HRIS Sync Mapping for the Position field. Map it to an unused standard element field ; for example, Customizable Field 7 can be used for this purpose. Once the mapping is done, click on Save.

3.3. Go to HRIS sync mapping check your added field weather is appearing or not.

3.4. Once HRIS Sync job will run the field data automatically available in the UDF. After that you can use this created Customizable field 7 “Position” in configure header fields.

Step 4: Save the general settings

Step 5: Navigate to the Employee Profile and Verify that the Position Code is now displayed along with the Position Name in the header

Conclusion

This workaround restores the visibility of the position code in the latest People Profile header by leveraging header field configuration and HRIS Sync Mapping. As a result, HR users and consultants can quickly identify positions without navigating to the Position Org Chart, improving efficiency and overall user experience.

entry.title: OData $batch Deleting Records Across Multiple Tables in Single Transaction
entry.content:
Introduction

 

Instead of sending each operation individually, OData batch processing enables you to transmit several operations (such as create, update, or delete) in a single request. This implies that you can use a single HTTP call to the $batch endpoint in SAP Gateway to execute actions on several tables at once.

For instance, you can make a single request to remove both a header record and the item records that are associated with it. When several records need to be managed as a single unit, this is helpful.

Batch processing enhances performance by minimizing network calls and ensures data consistency through change sets, which execute all operations as a single transactional unit. In case of any failure, the entire transaction is rolled back, thereby preventing partial data modifications.

Business Scenario

In the Manage Supplier Master application, a supplier’s complete data is not stored in a single table. Instead, it is distributed across multiple tables:

  • Basic Data (Supplier Name, Type)
  • Address Data
  • Identification Data (PAN, GST, etc.)
  • Purchasing Organization Data

Problem Statement

In the Manage Supplier Master application, when a user deletes a supplier, the system must remove all associated records from multiple backend tables, including basic details, address information, identification records, and purchasing organization data.

However, handling these deletions individually leads to several challenges:

  • Multiple HTTP calls are triggered for each table deletion
  • Increased network latency and poor performance
  • Risk of partial deletion (e.g., one table deleted, others not)
  • Data inconsistency across the system.

Prerequisites:

Before you implement batch delete in OData, make sure the following things are ready:

1. OData(V2) Service Setup: 

You should already have an SAP Gateway OData (v2) service created and registered.
All required tables (like ZTable1 , ZTable2) must be exposed as entity sets and should support DELETE operation.
Also, make sure runtime objects (Data Provider Class and Model) are generated using SEGW or CDS.

2. Entity Set Configuration:

Each table should be defined as an entity set with a key field.
In the service metadata (EDMX), batch processing ($batch) is available by default in OData v2.

3. Methods Implementation:

In the Data Provider Class:

  • Implement DELETE_ENTITY method for each entity (table).
  • Also implement:
    • CHANGESET_BEGIN
    • CHANGESET_PROCESS
    • CHANGESET_END

These help handle batch operations properly.

By default, only one delete is allowed per change set.
If you want multiple deletes in one go (same transaction), you need to enable deferred mode .

  METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~changeset_begin.
    cv_defer_mode = abap_true.
  ENDMETHOD.

Why do we use deferred mode in OData batch processing?

Normal Behavior (Without Deferred Mode)

By default in SAP Gateway:

  • Each operation in a change set is handled individually
  • Only one DELETE/UPDATE/CREATE is allowed per change set
  • Each operation is processed immediately

So we cannot group multiple deletes in one transaction

With Deferred Mode

When you enable deferred mode:

  • All operations in the change set are collected first
  • Then they are processed together in CHANGESET_PROCESS
  • You can handle multiple deletes/updates in one go
  • Deferred mode ensures that all operations in a batch are executed sequentially in a controlled manner without interruption, as part of a single transaction.

This allows you to treat them as one logical unit (single LUW)

Implementation

Step 1:   Creating Database table

Zdt_mini_akbasic( basic information table for the supplier) 

Zdt_mini_ak_add( address table for the supplier) 

Zdt_mini_a_ident( Identity table for the Supplier )

Zdt_mini_a_porg( Purchase organization table )

Step 2:  Now need to create the OData project with all the tables/Structure

METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~changeset_process.

  DATA: ls_changeset_request  TYPE /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_types=>ty_s_changeset_request,
        ls_changeset_response TYPE /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_types=>ty_s_changeset_response,
        lv_supplier_no        TYPE zde_mini_snumber,
        lv_request_line       TYPE string,
        ls_header             TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_name_value_pair,
        lo_busi_exception     TYPE REF TO /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception,
        lv_msg                TYPE string.

  LOOP AT it_changeset_request INTO ls_changeset_request.

    TRY.
        IF ls_changeset_request-operation_type = text-001.

          " Read each request header (name/value) and look for DELETE line
          LOOP AT ls_changeset_request-request_headers INTO ls_header.
             lv_request_line = |{ls_header-name} {ls_header-value}|.

            " Try to extract SupplierNumber from this line
            FIND PCRE 'SuppilerNumber=''([^'']+)''' " New syntax for Regular Expression
                 IN lv_request_line
                 SUBMATCHES lv_supplier_no.
            IF lv_supplier_no IS NOT INITIAL.
              EXIT. " Stop searching headers once we found the key
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.

          IF lv_supplier_no IS INITIAL.
            MESSAGE e000(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
            lo_busi_exception = NEW /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception( ).
            lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
              iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
              iv_msg_text =  CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
            ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
          ENDIF.

          " Delete from Basic Data
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            DELETE FROM zdt_mini_akbasic
              WHERE suppiler_number = lv_supplier_no.
          ELSE.
            MESSAGE e002(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
            lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
            iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
            iv_msg_text = CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
            ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
          ENDIF.

          " Delete from Address
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            DELETE FROM zdt_mini_ak_add
              WHERE suppiler_number = lv_supplier_no.
          ELSE.
            MESSAGE e003(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
            lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
            iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
            iv_msg_text = CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
            ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
          ENDIF.

          " Delete from Identification
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            DELETE FROM zdt_mini_a_ident
              WHERE suppiler_number = lv_supplier_no.
          ELSE.
            MESSAGE e004(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
            lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
            iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
            iv_msg_text = CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
            ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
          ENDIF.

          " Delete from Purchasing Org
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            DELETE FROM zdt_mini_a_porg
              WHERE suppiler_number = lv_supplier_no.
          ELSE.
            MESSAGE e005(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
            lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
            iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
            iv_msg_text = CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
            ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.

        " Maintain operation number in the response
        ls_changeset_response-operation_no = ls_changeset_request-operation_no.
        APPEND ls_changeset_response TO ct_changeset_response.
      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
        MESSAGE e000(zakmini) INTO lv_msg.
        lo_busi_exception->get_msg_container( )->add_message_text_only(
        iv_msg_type = zif_ak_constant=>c_msgty_error
        iv_msg_text = CONV bapi_msg( lv_msg )
        ).
        RAISE EXCEPTION lo_busi_exception.
    ENDTRY.

    CLEAR: ls_changeset_request, ls_changeset_response, lv_supplier_no, lv_request_line.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.
  method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~CHANGESET_END.
    COMMIT WORK.
  endmethod.

What COMMIT WORK Actually Does

  • Saves all successful operations to database
  • Ends the LUW (Logical Unit of Work)
  • Makes changes visible to other users.

Output:

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset


--changeset
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary


DELETE zakBasicSet(SupplierNumber='S0038') HTTP/1.1


--changeset--
--batch--

Conclusion:

  1. Once the $batch request is received, SAP Gateway identifies the changeset and starts processing it as a single transaction (LUW).
  2. The framework then reads each operation inside the changeset and calls the respective method.
  3. All operations inside the changeset are executed sequentially (one by one) but within the same transaction context.
  4. The backend logic performs all related table deletions, but no database commit happens immediately during execution.
  5. After all operations are successfully completed, COMMIT WORK is triggered automatically; if any operation fails, the entire changeset is rolled back, ensuring no partial data is saved.

entry.title: Exploring AI-Driven Finance Use Cases: Seeking Inputs from Experts
entry.content:
In today’s rapidly evolving business landscape, Finance functions are undergoing a major transformation driven by data, automation, and Artificial Intelligence (AI). As part of the CIS Innovation Incubation team, we are working on building AI-driven use cases to enhance efficiency, improve decision-making, and deliver value to customers and partners.

Why Finance?
Finance plays a critical role in every organization, covering areas such as financial planning, accounts management, risk, compliance, and forecasting. However, many processes still involve manual effort, disconnected systems, and delayed insights.

Our Objective
We are focusing on:

  • Identifying real-world pain points in Finance processes
  • Understanding customer and partner challenges
  • Co-creating impactful AI-driven solutions

Potential Areas of Innovation

  • Automated anomaly detection in financial data
  • Intelligent forecasting using predictive analytics
  • AI-based document processing for invoices and records
  • Conversational assistants for quick insights
  • Real-time risk and compliance monitoring

Collaboration Approach

  • Engaging with Product Managers and Finance teams
  • Conducting discussions to gather inputs and pain points
  • Running initial development in parallel for quick prototyping

Call for Inputs
We would like to hear from the community:

  • What are the key challenges you face in Finance today?
  • Which processes are time-consuming or error-prone?
  • Where do you see the highest potential for AI adoption?

Your inputs will help us build meaningful and scalable solutions.

Next Steps
Based on the feedback, we will prioritize use cases, develop prototypes, and iterate with stakeholders.

Conclusion
AI has the potential to transform Finance from reactive reporting to proactive decision-making. Collaboration will be key to achieving this transformation.

Looking forward to your inputs.

entry.title: Inside SAP IT 2026: Real Stories, Real Lessons, Real SAP – Live on YouTube
entry.content:
SAP runs SAP

It’s more than a slogan—it’s a commitment. SAP’s own IT organization (CPIT) acts as Customer Zero, adopting and validating SAP innovations before they reach the market. This means every new capability, every architectural shift, and every AI breakthrough is tested first inside one of the world’s most complex enterprise landscapes.

In 2026, we’re taking you behind the scenes again with a new season of Inside SAP IT, our live YouTube series where SAP experts share unfiltered insights, lessons learned, and the real stories behind SAP’s internal transformation.

Authentic. Practical. Unscripted.

 

Whether you’re an IT leader, architect, developer, or business transformation professional, this series gives you a front‑row seat to how SAP builds, scales, and operates the technologies shaping the intelligent, agentic enterprise.

⭐ What to expect from this season

Across ten episodes, SAP IT leaders and experts will unpack how SAP is:

  • Running agentic AI at enterprise scale
  • Modernizing core processes with SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, SAP Sales Cloud, SAP CPQ, and SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Operationalizing data products in SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Reinventing support, finance, HR, and revenue operations with AI agents
  • Delivering intelligent experiences through SAP for Me and SAP’s internal platforms
  • Transforming planning, treasury, travel & expense, and more

Each session is live, interactive, and openly shares what worked, what didn’t, and what others can learn from SAP’s journey.

 📅Inside SAP IT – 2026 Live Series Schedule

Episode 1


 

 

 

 

Date: Thu, Apr 23 – 15:00 CEST

SAP opens the 2026 season with a rare inside look at how one of the world’s most complex IT landscapes is run, evolved, and continuously reinvented. Hear directly from SAP’s CPIO on what it means to act as Customer Zero, how agentic AI is reshaping work, and what keeps SAP’s IT leadership awake at night.

  • Hear what skills tomorrow’s IT leaders need now

entry.title: Re: SAP BW Bridge or Data Product Generator (DPG): What would you choose
entry.content:
Hello Ankit,

I am sure you don't necessarily need BW Bridge to connect an S/4 on premise ODP extractor to the Datasphere. I am partly still using old extractors (like 0FI_AR_4, because SAP has not yet provided a CDS extractor for this). We are running a cloud connector service and the DP Agent for all purposes where we can't load data via CDS, without using BW Bridge.

Of course you still need a BW Bridge if your logic is on BW side.

Kind regards Jochen

entry.title: SAP ASE - Rolling SPS UPGRADE in HADR System
entry.content:
The HADR feature allows SAP ASE applications to operate with zero downtime while you are updating the SAP ASE software.

     Generally, the steps for performing a rolling upgrade are:

  • Upgrade the standby components
  • Perform a failover
  • Upgrade the components that are on standby after the failover

Complete the upgrade steps in a single sequence: partial upgrade is not supported (for example, you cannot upgrade some components now and then upgrade the other components at another time). Replication is suspended during some steps of a rolling upgrade, and if you perform a partial upgrade, logs continue to grow, which can result in logs or the SPQ running out of space. In the course of a rolling upgrade, it is not required for the SAP ASE and Replication Server versions to be identical.

In this blog we upgraded the SAP ASE version from 16.0 SP03 PL09 to 16.0 SP04 PL06. Also Let’s assume that we have following sites names for Primary, Secondary and DR Location.

Primary – Site1                                  Secondary – Site2                              DR – Site3

We have few prerequisites below which we need to consider before starting the upgrade.

isql -Usa -P <password> -S<hostname>:<port> -X -w999
sp_version
go

sp_configure “enable java”
go

sp_configure “auditing”
go

sp_lmconfig

Repeat the steps for Secondary and DR and collect the screenshots.

Log into the DR node's RMA as the user DR admin using isql , and issue below command to start the upgrade process and then shut down RMA

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMA Port> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, start, Site3(DR)

shutdown
go

Install the new version of Replication Server using the setup.bin utility. Go to download directory extract the software using SAPCAR and run setup.bin

Check if process is running

 

Exit the installer and start RMA
Go to below location and run the command ./rma & to start the RMA
Location- /Sybase/<SID>/ASE-16_0/bin

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, finish, Site3(DR)

 
Post upgrade check replication status
sap status path

Upgrade the SAP ASE database at DR Node
isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, start, site3

Shutdown backup Server

isql_r64 -Usa -P<Password> -S<SID> -x -w999
shutdown SYB_ BACKUP
go 


Shutdown ASE Server
shutdown
go 

Install the new version of ASE Server using the setup.bin utility. Go to download directory extract the software using SAPCAR and run setup.bin


After the SAP installer has completed the upgrade, use the updatease utility to upgrade SAP ASE, which runs installmaster and performs other tasks to bring SAP ASE up to date. updatease is available as a GUI display or a command line tool.


Enter the Server name <SID> and password <sa user> 

Finished the upgrade the SAP ASE database at DR Node
isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, finish, site3

Stop the Fault Manager before you perform a rolling upgrade (even if you are performing planned activities like a planned failover). You can start the Fault Manager after the upgrade is complete. To stop the Fault Manager, issue this from the <installation_directory>/FaultManager directory:


<Fault_Manager_install_dir>/FaultManager/bin/sybdbfm stop


Log into the Primary node RMA as the user DR_admin using isql , and issue below command to start the upgrade process and then shutdown RMA

isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<port> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, start, Site1(Primary)


shutdown
go


check RMA process: 
ps -ef |grep repserver 
ps -ef |grep rma


Once Sybase Replication Server (SRS) upgrade finished exit the installer and start RMA
Go to below location and run the command ./rma & to start the RMA
Location- /Sybase/<SID>/ASE-16_0/bin

Finish the SRS upgrade with below command.

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<port> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, finish, Site1(Primary)


Perform Failover from Site1 (Primary) to Site2 (Secondary) – Log into RMA on site1 as the DR_admin user and issue:

sap_failover <site1 >, <site2 >, 30

This command allows a 30-second grace period for any running transactions to complete before the deactivation starts. Failover will not succeed if there are still active transactions after 30 seconds. If this occurs, retry the command when the system is not busy, use a longer grace period, or use the force option to terminate the client connection (if it is safe) with:

sap_failover <site1 >, <site2 >, 30, force

The sap_failover command may take a long time to finish. To check the status of the sap_failover command, issue this from the RMA:
sap_status task

 

Use the sap_failover_drain_to_dr command while performing failover inside an HADR with DR node environment. The sap_failover_drain_to_dr command makes sure that the incremental backlog from the HA cluster is drained to the DR node.

sap_failover_drain_to_dr 30

sap_status task

Once the sap_status command returns Completed, resume replication by issuing this from the RMA:
sap_host_available <site1> (Primary)
sap_host_available <site3> (DR)

Log into the Secondary node’s RMA as the user DR admin using isql, and issue below command to start the upgrade process and then shutdown RMA

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, start, Site2(Secondary)

shutdown
go


check RMA process: 
ps -ef |grep repserver 
ps -ef |grep rma


Once Sybase Replication Server (SRS) upgrade finished exit the installer and start RMA
Go to below location and run the command ./rma & to start the RMA
Location- /Sybase/<SID>/ASE-16_0/bin

Finish the SRS upgrade with below command.

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<port> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server SRS, finish, Site2(Secondary)

Upgrade the Primary node's (SITE1) SAP ASE database.

isql -U DR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, start, site1(Primary)

isql_r64 -Usa -P<Password> -S<SID> -x -w999
shutdown SYB_ BACKUP
go
 
Shutdown ASE Server

shutdown
go


Install the new version of ASE Server using the setup.bin utility. Go to download directory extract the software using SAPCAR and run setup.bin
Run the updatease utility to upgrade SAP ASE,  Enter the Server name <SID> and password <sa user>

Finished the upgrade the SAP ASE database at Site1 Node

isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, finish, site1 (Primary)

Failback to Site1 – Login on current Primary Database (Site2)
isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_failover <site2 >, <site1 >, 30

sap_status task
sap_failover_drain_to_dr 30
sap_status task
sap_host_available <site2> (Secondary)
sap_host_available <site3> (DR)


Upgrade the Secondary node's (SITE2) SAP ASE database.

isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, start, site2(Secondary)

isql_r64 -Usa -P<Password> -S<SID> -x -w999
shutdown SYB_ BACKUP
go
 
Shutdown ASE Server

shutdown
go


Install the new version of ASE Server using the setup.bin utility. Go to download directory extract the software using SAPCAR and run setup.bin
Run the updatease utility to upgrade SAP ASE, Enter the Server name <SID> and password <sa user>

Finished the upgrade the SAP ASE database at Site1 Node

isql -UDR_admin -P <password> -S<hostname>:<RMAPort> -X -w999
sap_upgrade_server ASE, finish, site2 (Secondary)

This blog help us to understand an approach for upgrade sap ASE (SYBASE) in HADR environment
